title: corymbia cadophora subsp. pliantha
intro: Corymbia cadophora subsp. pliantha, also known as the Western Australian bloodwood, is a species of tree native to Western Australia. The medicinal properties of this tree include its bark, which contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with Corymbia cadophora subsp. pliantha include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditional herbal preparations involve using the bark to make teas or infusions, which can be used to support immune function and overall health.
